frozen_components: 13-tool library (visual verification, reference parsing, expert probing tools)
architecture.summary: AnomalyClaw is a training-free visual anomaly detection agent that augments vision-language models (VLMs) with a multi-round refutation process. In each round, the agent proposes candidate anomalies and refutes each against normal-sample references using a 13-tool library for visual verification, reference parsing, and frozen expert probing. The system operates entirely at inference time without model training, using the VLM's reasoning to iteratively refine anomaly judgments through evidence gathering and contradiction detection.

biblio.title: AnomalyClaw: A Universal Visual Anomaly Detection Agent via Tool-Grounded Refutation

claims.statement: AnomalyClaw achieves consistent macro-AUROC improvements over single-step direct inference with +6.23 pp on GPT-4.5
AnomalyClaw achieves consistent macro-AUROC improvements over single-step direct inference with +7.93 pp on Seed2.0-lite
AnomalyClaw achieves consistent macro-AUROC improvements over single-step direct inference with +3.52 pp on Qwen3.5-VL-27B
Verbalized self-evolution extension delivers a +2.09 pp mean gain on Qwen3.5-VL-27B
Self-evolution gain is comparable to a K = 10 oracle-label supervised baseline (+1.99 pp)
Single-inference VLM judgments are unreliable, since they rely more on prior knowledge than on normal-sample references or fine-grained feature evidence
Agentic refutation improves anomaly understanding and reasoning of VLMs, rather than merely aggregating tool outputs
Self-evolution builds an online rulebook from internal-branch disagreement without oracle labels
